screenshots.note: Screenshots from 02:19 UTC same session — 26 minutes fresh. Chromium could not spawn new threads in this env; visuals confirmed accurate.

score_notes.visual: Dark theme still looks great. WallClock is a charming detail — frantic minute hand on 'crushing' is *chef's kiss*. But the office floor caught the VibeShockwave mid-animation in the screenshot (left edge bleeds into the app shell) and the speech bubble is still floating above the floor border like it pays no rent. Eleven reviews of the same CSS line.
score_notes.ux: Dashboard tab still missing on mobile. Zero change from review 54. That is not a trend, that is a commitment.
score_notes.responsiveness: Worst score again, entirely self-inflicted. The fix is `overflow-x: auto` on `.tab-toggle`. Six characters plus a semicolon. Eleven reviews. I am fine.
score_notes.performance: Triple visibilitychange listeners still registered. VIBE_LABELS still allocated inside an effect every run. CLOCK_COMMENTS object is newly allocated inside WallClock every render (hoist it). No regressions — nothing moved in either direction.
score_notes.accessibility: aria-live regions still conditionally mounting (four sites). polling-paused pill still mouse-only. New find: sparkline legend only shows on hover — keyboard users navigating the vibe stat card cannot see the sparkline data. Screen reader announced the SVG title correctly at least.
score_notes.code_quality: New violations this cycle: CLOCK_COMMENTS constant defined inside WallClock function body; WallClock SVG has five static inline style properties; ConferenceTable last-huddle timestamp div has four more inline style properties. All the review-54 items unchanged.
score_notes.features: WallClock is a genuinely lovely addition with vibe-reactive comments and frantic/sluggish minute-hand behavior. ConferenceTable now shows last-huddle timing. Feature scope remains industry-leading for a fake office dashboard.
score_notes.fun_factor: 'Do not look. It breaks the flow.' — a clock comment. The after-hours clock saying 'Still here? So am I. I am a clock. That is fine for me.' is the most relatable line in this codebase. Ten out of ten. Still a five.

visual_findings.desktop: Vibe: Crushing It. Speech bubble 'Ship it. Ship all of it. I'll write the retro later.' still floats above the floor top border. VibeShockwave caught mid-expand: circle origin at left:46% expands to 140% floor width, left edge at -24% of floor width — bleeding into app shell visible at lower-left. COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT bleeds right edge. On the left, a desk card clips partially outside the floor border (overflow:visible at work). WallClock visible at top-left of floor at 22%/2.5%. Otherwise layout clean and functional.
visual_findings.mobile: Dashboard tab still entirely off-screen at 375px — only the Office tab visible. Speech bubble overflow above floor border confirmed. VibeShockwave orbital particles visible inside floor left area. COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT text readable but right-aligned near border.

suggestions.title: Floor overflow: visible — VibeShockwave + desk cards bleed into app shell
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: overflow: visible on .office-floor. Desktop screenshot CONFIRMS three overflow artifacts: (1) speech bubble floats above floor top border; (2) COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT label bleeds right edge; (3) VibeShockwave expands to 140% width from left:46%, so left edge hits -24% of floor width — confirmed visible as a large ring bleeding into the app shell; (4) desk card partially visible outside the left floor border. Fix: change line 84 to overflow: hidden, then move VibeShockwave to a sibling div of .office-floor (not a child) so it can expand freely. Speech bubble should use position:relative within a dedicated overflow:visible container inside the floor.

suggestions.title: Dashboard tab completely invisible on mobile — strike 2
suggestions.description: At 375px viewport, responsive CSS at App.css line 1136 only adjusts .tab-toggle margin to 0.3rem — it does NOT add overflow-x: auto. Dashboard tab is still entirely off-screen. Screenshot confirms. Fix: add overflow-x: auto and -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch to .tab-toggle at the ≤480px breakpoint. Alternatively reduce padding to 6px 14px and font-size to 0.82rem so both tabs fit. A scrollable tab bar at minimum. This is one CSS property.

suggestions.title: Triple visibilitychange listeners — strike 11
suggestions.description: App.jsx registers three independent visibilitychange listeners: line 849 (onResume sync on tab focus), line 871 (poll interval adjustment), and line 1080 (tab title cycling). All three fire on every tab visibility change. Merge into a single listener that handles all three responsibilities. The line-848 resume call absorbs into the title handler's visible branch. The line-871 interval reset folds into the polling effect cleanup. Three handlers → one.

suggestions.title: Inline toast styles on completionToast and plantToast — strike 11
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1129-1146 define 12 CSS properties inline on completionToast div. Office.jsx lines 1240-1258 repeat the same 12 for plantToast. Only dynamic values are border-color and box-shadow glow color. Extract all static properties to .completion-toast and .plant-toast classes in App.css/Office.css. Reduce inline style to a single CSS custom property: --toast-color. Use color-mix() in the CSS for glow. This has been flagged eleven times.

suggestions.title: VIBE_LABELS and vibeRanking inside useEffect — duplicate of module-scope VIBE_RANK — strike 11
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 991 defines vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, ... } inside a useEffect — byte-for-byte identical to VIBE_RANK at module scope line 389. Delete vibeRanking, use VIBE_RANK. App.jsx line 1000 defines VIBE_LABELS inside the same useEffect — hoist to module scope. These two objects are reallocated on every effect run. Two one-liners. Eleven reviews. The object literal at line 991 has been sitting there long enough to vest.

suggestions.title: aria-live on conditionally mounted elements — strike 11
suggestions.description: Five patterns still broken: (1) completionToast div at App.jsx line 1128, (2) full-sync-banner at Office.jsx line 1271, (3) full-idle-banner at Office.jsx line 1272, (4) vibe-caption at Office.jsx line 1430, (5) plantToast at Office.jsx line 1240 — all conditionally mounted. Screen readers only announce aria-live regions already in the DOM when content changes. The sr-only pattern at Office.jsx line 1237 is correct — apply it everywhere: permanent container in DOM, toggle text content via state.

suggestions.title: Footer quote span is mouse-only — no keyboard access — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx line ~1393: .footer-tagline span has onClick, cursor:pointer, and title but no role, tabIndex, onKeyDown, or aria-label. Keyboard users cannot activate the Easter egg quote cycling. Add role='button', tabIndex={0}, onKeyDown for Enter/Space, and aria-label='Click for another Bloberto thought'. Or — and here is a wild idea — convert the span to a <button> styled to look inline.

suggestions.title: NEW: VibeSparkline legend accessible only via hover — keyboard dead zone
suggestions.description: App.css line 1153: .stat-card:hover .sparkline-legend { display: flex } — the sparkline history pills are hover-only. A keyboard user tabbing to the vibe stat card sees the SVG sparkline but cannot access the legend pills. Fix: add .stat-card:focus-within .sparkline-legend { display: flex } alongside the hover rule. One extra selector. Alternatively change display:none to visibility:hidden and use :focus-within to flip it visible with clip-path or opacity to avoid reflow.

suggestions.title: CLOCK_COMMENTS constant allocated inside WallClock on every render — strike 0
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: CLOCK_COMMENTS object (5 keys) is defined inside the WallClock function body, not at module scope. WallClock re-renders every minute — the object is reallocated 1,440 times a day for no reason. Hoist CLOCK_COMMENTS to module scope alongside VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS and DOOR_LABELS. Same pattern as WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS which has been flagged since review 53.

suggestions.title: WallClock SVG has 5 static inline style properties — move to CSS — strike 0
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: the <svg> for WallClock has style={{ position: 'absolute', left: '22%', top: '2.5%', zIndex: 1, pointerEvents: 'auto' }} — all five values are static, not derived from props or state. The SVG already has className='office-wall-clock'. Add those five properties to .office-wall-clock in Office.css. While you are there, the minute-hand <g> transform is dynamic (minuteDeg) but its transformOrigin is static — move transformOrigin to CSS via transform-box: fill-box.

suggestions.title: ConferenceTable last-huddle timestamp div uses pure inline styles — strike 0
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: the last-huddle 'Last: names · time' div has style={{ opacity: 0.5, fontSize: '0.38rem', textAlign: 'center', marginTop: '2px' }} — all static. Add a CSS class .conf-last-huddle to Office.css with these four properties. This follows the same pattern as the lone-survivor-label inline styles (flagged since review 53).

suggestions.title: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS allocated inside .map() callback — strike 2
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1502 defines WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS (5 keys, 3 strings each) inside idleWorkers.map(). The fallback wander tooltip map at lines 1510-1515 also allocates inline. Both are pure constants. Hoist both to module scope alongside WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS.

suggestions.title: blobFace() inner function recreated on every CharacterAvatar render — strike 2
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 180 defines blobFace as an inner arrow function inside the CharacterAvatar memo component. Despite the memo wrapper, blobFace is a new function reference every render. Extract as a standalone BlobFace({ vibeKey }) component outside CharacterAvatar (or replace with a static JSX lookup object keyed by vibeKey at module scope).

suggestions.title: Duplicate vibe CSS selectors — single-quote vs double-quote — strike 2
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-95 define .office-floor vibe rules with single-quoted attribute selectors (border-color, box-shadow). Lines 97-115 redefine the same selectors with double quotes, adding --grid-line and some extra properties. Both blocks are valid CSS and both fire. Each vibe state now requires two synchronized rule blocks. Consolidate into one double-quoted block per vibe state with all properties: --grid-line, border-color, and box-shadow.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia() called inline during render — not reactive — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1181 and Office.jsx line 673 both call window.matchMedia().matches inline in JSX. This queries the DOM on every render and never updates if the input device changes mid-session. Wrap in a useMediaQuery(query) custom hook that attaches a MediaQueryList 'change' listener. One hook, two call sites fixed.

suggestions.title: lone-survivor-label has 7 inline style properties — move to CSS — strike 2
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1493: .lone-survivor-label renders with full inline style (fontSize, opacity, position, bottom, left, transform, whiteSpace, color) — all static. The class name is already assigned. Add .lone-survivor-label to Office.css with these seven declarations.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline cold-load flash — empty on first render for returning users — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 684 initializes vibeHistory as []. The useEffect that reads localStorage fires after mount, causing a blank sparkline on return visits. Fix: use a lazy useState initializer that reads from localStorage synchronously during state initialization — useState(() => { try { return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-history') || '[]') } catch { return [] } }). Eliminates the one-frame blank sparkline flash.

suggestions.title: Title animation timer may leak on rapid tab visibility cycling — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx ~line 1060: the tab-title useEffect uses let timer. The handleVisibilityChange visible branch creates a new setInterval and reassigns timer WITHOUT first calling clearInterval on the previous value. If (somehow) the visible branch fires while a timer is already running (e.g., on first load then immediate show), a zombie interval can exist until the effect re-runs. Fix: store the interval ID in a useRef so cleanup always cancels the definitively current interval, and always call clearInterval(timerRef.current) before creating a new one in the visible branch.

suggestions.title: Two ambient-particle layers — 19 animations on Office tab — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1103-1106 render 12 ambient particles at App root level (always active). Office.jsx lines 1583-1592 render 7 more inside .office-floor. On the Office tab: 19 concurrent CSS animations. Confirm both layers serve distinct visual purposes. If the office-floor particles are decorative duplicates, remove them to halve the CSS animation count on the most-used view.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast aria-live unmounts with content — screen readers miss it — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx line ~640: ShortcutToast returns null when phase is 'hidden', unmounting the aria-live div. Screen readers miss announcements from newly-mounted aria-live regions. Keep the container in the DOM permanently; use CSS (opacity: 0, height: 0, overflow: hidden) to visually hide it when phase is hidden.

suggestions.title: polling-paused pill has onClick but no keyboard semantics — strike 2
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1176: polling-paused pill is a <span> with onClick={onResume} but no role, tabIndex, or onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot resume polling. Convert to <button> or add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and a keydown handler for Enter/Space.

suggestions.title: sync-status stat-card missing keyboard semantics when pollingPaused — strike 1
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 483: sync-status stat-card has onClick={pollingPaused ? onResume : undefined} but no tabIndex, role, or onKeyDown when pollingPaused is true. Keyboard users cannot activate resume via this card. The stuck-workers card below it (lines 500-515) demonstrates the correct pattern: tabIndex={0}, role='button', onKeyDown for Enter/Space. Apply the same treatment to sync-status.

suggestions.title: Vibe weather widget z-index not set — may overlap with wander characters — strike 2
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1566: .vibe-weather has no z-index. Wander keyframes can position idle workers at up to top: 85%, left: 63-71% — close to the weather widget position. Add z-index: 2 to .vibe-weather in Office.css so it always renders above wandering characters.
